methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Rain AI is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Gordon Wilson, currently valued at $500M post-money after raising $146M to date. The valuation is benchmarked against a peer universe of 3 public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip companies, with primary comparables including Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and Substrate ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $500M last post-money, implies $1.0B at the next financing stage. Peer-median anchoring across the 3 same-sector, same-stage private comps yields $2.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $5.0B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue figures. The wide peer range ($1.0B–$5.0B) reflects heterogeneous chip-company valuations driven by differing capital intensity and commercialization timelines.

Triangulating across these two methods produces a valuation range of $850M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.5B. This suggests the current $500M valuation reflects early-stage risk and capital efficiency; the median implies material upside in a subsequent round absent material setbacks. Key uncertainties include technical execution risk, competitive positioning relative to Etched and d-Matrix, and path to material revenue generation.
